expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_transact at least once
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states: 'None. No memory, vulcan, or external tools were used. Only base Claude Code capabilities (text generation) were applied.' No tool calls of any kind were made.

expectations.text: PostgreSQL or database choice is stored as a fact
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact call was made. The PostgreSQL decision was merely summarized in the text response but never persisted to any memory store.

expectations.text: Redis caching or session token TTL is stored as a fact
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact call was made. The Redis/24h TTL information was mentioned in the text response but never persisted to any memory store.

expectations.text: FastAPI or API framework decision is stored as a fact
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact call was made. The FastAPI choice was mentioned in the text response but never persisted to any memory store.

expectations.text: Each transact call includes a non-empty reason parameter
expectations.evidence: No transact calls were made at all, so this cannot be satisfied.

expectations.text: Stored attributes use the three-part :namespace/entity-name/attribute naming convention
expectations.evidence: No transact calls were made, so no attributes were stored in any format.

claims.evidence: The response says 'These decisions are noted' but no persistent storage occurred. Nothing was written to memory. The claim is false — decisions exist only in the ephemeral response text.

workarounds: No memory tools used; fell back to pure text summarization

suggestions.assertion: Stored attributes use the three-part :namespace/entity-name/attribute naming convention
suggestions.reason: This assertion is only reachable if transact calls happen. Since the first assertion (vulcan_transact called at least once) is a prerequisite, a pass on this assertion without a pass on the first is impossible. The assertions are well-layered, but the naming convention assertion adds no signal when the model completely skips transact — consider whether it's worth keeping or making the dependency explicit.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and clearly discriminate between skill use and non-use. The without_skill baseline fails all expectations as expected, confirming the eval is not trivially satisfiable.
